description: ThousandEyes capabilities for healthcare organizations including hospitals, health systems, telehealth providers, and medical device companies

common_requirements.answer: ThousandEyes maintains SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certification; HIPAA compliance requires Business Associate Agreement (BAA) which should be confirmed with Cisco.
ThousandEyes supports HITECH Act requirements through encryption, audit logging, and access controls; formal compliance requires BAA with Cisco.
Yes. ThousandEyes maintains SOC 2 Type II certification demonstrating comprehensive security controls and practices.
Yes. ThousandEyes is ISO 27001 certified for information security management systems.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides comprehensive audit logging for user actions, configuration changes, and system events for compliance tracking.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors Epic applications including MyChart, EpicCare, and Hyperspace through HTTP/API testing and synthetic transactions.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors Cerner Millennium and patient portals through synthetic testing for availability and performance metrics.
Partial. ThousandEyes monitors EHR/EMR system performance and availability through HTTP/API testing but does not integrate with EHR databases directly.
Partial. ThousandEyes monitors HL7 interfaces through API testing for availability and response time but does not parse or validate HL7 message content.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides full transaction testing for FHIR endpoints including availability, response time, and API validation.
Partial. ThousandEyes monitors HL7 interface endpoints for performance but does not validate HL7 message structure or content compliance.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors telehealth platforms including Amwell and Teladoc through synthetic testing and user experience monitoring.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors video quality through VoIP/RTP metrics including MOS scores, jitter, latency, and packet loss.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors patient portal performance including page load times, transaction completion, and user experience metrics.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors MyChart patient portal for availability, performance, and user experience through synthetic testing.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides end-to-end network monitoring across multiple hospital locations with path visualization and performance metrics.
Partial. ThousandEyes monitors network paths to medical devices but does not monitor the devices themselves; focuses on network connectivity.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides real-time monitoring with one-second granularity, configurable alerts, and 99.9%+ SLA for critical healthcare applications.
Yes. ThousandEyes Endpoint Agents monitor VPN tunnel performance including latency, packet loss, and connection quality for remote workers.
Yes. ThousandEyes Endpoint Agents measure WiFi signal strength, channel quality, roaming events, and connection performance in real-time.
Yes. ThousandEyes integrates natively with ServiceNow for automated incident creation, event management, and CMDB correlation.
Yes. ThousandEyes integrates with Splunk and can send data to SIEM platforms via API and webhooks for security monitoring.
Yes. ThousandEyes sends real-time alerts to Slack and Microsoft Teams through native integrations.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides customizable dashboards with role-based access control for different stakeholder views.
Yes. ThousandEyes retains historical performance data with configurable retention periods for trend analysis and compliance.
Yes. ThousandEyes alerts trigger immediately upon threshold breach with notifications via email, webhook, SMS, and integration platforms.
Yes. ThousandEyes is a cloud-based SaaS platform with global infrastructure across 193+ locations.
Yes. ThousandEyes supports hybrid deployment with cloud-based platform and optional on-premise Enterprise Agents.
Yes. Cisco provides tiered support with 24/7 availability for critical issues based on subscription level.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors network connectivity, application performance, and user experience for distributed physician practices.
Yes. ThousandEyes Endpoint Agents monitor home health worker connectivity including WiFi, VPN, and application performance.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors PACS system availability, network path performance, and image retrieval times through HTTP/API testing.
